comments.reader: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in reading. She is developing her reading skills and approaches texts with determination. Asel's reading fluency is growing and she is becoming more confident in reading aloud with appropriate pace and accuracy. She can retrieve information from texts when prompted and is developing her ability to locate key details. The main area for Asel to focus on is inference — building her skills in drawing conclusions about characters' feelings and motivations from evidence in the text. This is a key skill that will unlock deeper comprehension for her. Asel's reading vocabulary is an important area for growth and will support both her understanding and her writing. Reading widely every day from engaging texts will be central to her continued progress in Year 5.
comments.writer: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in writing. She has made solid progress in developing her writing skills throughout Year 4 and approaches writing tasks with focus and determination. Asel's spelling is developing well and she is growing in confidence with the statutory word list and common spelling patterns. Her handwriting is becoming more joined and fluent, which supports her ability to record her ideas more efficiently. The key areas for Asel to develop further are her composition skills — building confidence in generating and organising ideas — and her use of punctuation and grammar to support meaning. Her vocabulary choices are an important next step, and drawing more actively on her reading will help enrich her writing. With continued focus on these areas, Asel will be able to show more of her potential as a writer in Year 5.
comments.mathematician: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in mathematics. She is a focused mathematician who brings strong effort to her learning and has made good progress throughout Year 4. Asel's multiplication tables knowledge is excellent — she knows her tables fluently and accurately, which is a real strength that supports her across many areas of mathematics. Her written calculation skills are developing well and she is growing in confidence with column methods for addition and subtraction. Asel is making good progress with fractions and decimals, building on her strong times table foundation. The main areas for her to develop are mathematical reasoning and problem solving — building confidence in explaining her thinking and tackling unfamiliar problems. These skills will become increasingly important in Year 5 as the mathematical demands increase.
comments.learner_21c: Asel, you have shown excellent character throughout Year 4 and your independence is one of your greatest strengths. You are very capable of directing your own learning and managing tasks without constant guidance, which is impressive. Your collaboration skills are strong and you work very well with others in the Maple Learning Zone. You are developing your effort and resilience — when you meet challenges, you are growing in your willingness to persist and push through difficulties. An important area for you to develop is your curiosity and your confidence to share your ideas and questions with others. You have thoughtful contributions to make and building your confidence to speak up will help you grow even more as a learner. Keep building on these qualities in Year 5, Asel.
comments.rights: Asel, you show an excellent understanding of your rights and responsibilities as a learner and this is evident in everything you do. You are consistently respectful and thoughtful, and you show real care for others around you. Your helpful nature contributes positively to our learning zone and you are always willing to support your classmates when needed. You listen carefully and are growing in your confidence to contribute your own ideas to discussions. These strong foundations will serve you very well in Year 5.

comments.reader: Haris has achieved the age-related standard in reading. He is a confident reader and can fluently read and comprehend texts at the appropriate, age-related, level. He has developing comprehension skills and is developing his ability to draw inference with increasing confidence, an important skill to continue building through Year 5. He is able to talk about the content of texts with growing confidence and shows real interest in a broad range of books. Haris's vocabulary is developing well and is beginning to support his written communication. Reading widely every day from quality texts will strengthen these skills as he moves into Year 5.
comments.writer: Haris has achieved the age-related standard in writing. He has focused on developing his writing skills across the year, with a focus on accuracy in grammar and punctuation. He is growing in confidence with the composition of his ideas and is beginning to make more varied vocabulary choices. Haris is developing his editing skills and, with continued attention to accuracy and clarity, will be able to show more of what he is capable of as a writer. In Year 5, using his reading as a regular source of vocabulary and ideas will deepen his writing further while continuing to strengthen accuracy in grammar, punctuation and spelling.
comments.mathematician: Haris has achieved the age-related standard in mathematics. He is a confident and accurate mathematician across the range of topics covered in Year 4. He consistently applies himself well and is able to use his fluency to solve problems with increasing confidence. Haris is developing his reasoning and problem-solving skills and is becoming more able to identify the approach needed when tackling a problem. Continuing to build his fluency with multiplication and division facts will further support him with calculations involving fractions and more complex problems in Year 5.
comments.learner_21c: Haris, you have made excellent progress as a 21st Century Learner throughout Year 4 and the effort you put in every day is something to be very proud of. You bring curiosity to your learning and are developing your confidence to ask questions and explore ideas further. Continuing to develop your resilience will be key. Being determined when facing challenges is where the most important learning happens. Strong independence and good collaboration are two qualities you demonstrate consistently. Both will serve you very well in Year 5. The depth of knowledge you bring to our learning is impressive and it clearly has a positive effect on everyone around you. Carry these qualities into Year 5, Haris and keep pushing to develop them. There is a great deal more ahead for you.
comments.rights: Haris, you show a good understanding of your rights and responsibilities as a learner and are consistent in your learning dispositions.You listen well to others and contribute to our discussions with respect and consideration, showing how much you value what everyone has to say. Your ability to maintain focus during learning is developing well and you are becoming increasingly able to manage challenges independently. Your friendly and approachable manner makes you someone who others genuinely enjoy learning with. Keep developing these qualities in Year 5, Haris. You have a lot to be proud of.

comments.reader: Amir has achieved the age-related standard in reading and in some aspects has been learning at greater depth. He reads fluently and expressively, using intonation and punctuation to support meaning and draw the listener into the text. He reads a wide range of fiction and non-fiction texts and engages confidently with challenging material. His vocabulary is a real strength, enriching both his comprehension and writing. He draws on his reading to deepen his knowledge across the curriculum. In Year 5, continuing to seek out ambitious and varied texts will build on the excellent foundations he has established this year.
comments.writer: Amir has achieved the age-related standard in writing. He is a developing author who has shown growing confidence and skill across different genres this year. He is secure with the key skills of grammar and punctuation and has growing confidence with spelling. Amir is beginning to draw on his reading to develop his vocabulary and composition, making more ambitious choices that reflect an awareness of his audience. Amir applies himself with real commitment to being a writer and this effort is reflected in his outcomes. Continuing to develop the precision of his word choices and revising his writing carefully will allow Amir to show the full range of his skills as an author in Year 5.
comments.mathematician: Amir has achieved the age-related standard in mathematics and in some aspects has been learning at greater depth. He is a focused and capable mathematician who applies himself fully in every lesson, building both his accuracy and his depth of understanding. Amir's strong fluency underpins his ability to tackle more complex and open-ended problems. He approaches unfamiliar problems with particular enthusiasm and determination and is willing to explore different approaches to reach a solution. Amir's mathematical reasoning is clear and well articulated and he contributes confidently in class discussions, supporting others while deepening his own understanding.
comments.learner_21c: Amir, your commitment to your learning throughout Year 4 has been clear to see and you are a genuine example of what it means to be a 21st Century Learner. Your curiosity is developing well and you are becoming more willing to ask questions and pursue ideas that deepen your understanding. You have excellent resilience. When you meet a challenge you do not give up and this determination is one of your greatest strengths. Your independence is a genuine strength and you are very capable of directing your own learning. You also collaborate effectively with others. Your love of problem solving and your willingness to think things through carefully is a real asset. In Year 5, Amir, keep nurturing these qualities. You have so much potential and we cannot wait to see more of it.
comments.rights: Amir, you have a good grasp of your rights and responsibilities as a learner and this is reflected in how you conduct yourself day to day.You listen well to others and are respectful in your interactions with both peers and adults. The effort and commitment you show in your learning is clear to everyone and is something that will take you a very long way. Your approachable and friendly manner makes you a popular and valued member of our community. Keep bringing that same commitment and care to Year 5, Amir. It makes a real difference.

comments.reader: Daisy has not yet achieved the age-related standard in reading and is having additional support and provision to develop her skills. She has shown real determination in approaching her reading this year and this effort is noticed and valued. The key focus for Daisy is on comprehension, building a stronger connection between her ability to read aloud and her understanding of what she has read. Reading every day at the right level of challenge, actively thinking about the meaning of texts, will be central to her continued progress in Year 5. We would love to see Daisy explore a wide range of books to build her vocabulary and confidence as a reader.
comments.writer: Daisy has not yet achieved the age-related standard in writing and is having additional support and provision to develop her skills. She has put real effort into the writing process this year and has growing enthusiasm for it. The main areas of focus are accuracy in spelling and punctuation and the clarity and cohesion of her ideas across a piece. Daisy is developing her ability to compose ideas over longer pieces and, with continued support and focus, will build the stamina and accuracy needed to show her full potential. In Year 5, reading widely and applying the key skills carefully will be central to her progress as a writer.
comments.mathematician: Daisy has achieved the age-related standard in mathematics. She has shown progress across Year 4 and is growing in confidence in the key areas of calculation. Daisy is developing her reasoning skills and is becoming more able to apply her number knowledge to solve problems. Building fluency with multiplication and division facts is an important priority in Year 5 as this will support both her accuracy and her confidence with more complex calculations. Regular practice and active engagement in all learning will be the key driver of her continued progress.

With continued support, Sam is developing the skills needed to write with greater confidence and independence. In Year 5, a continued focus on these core skills will be central to his progress as a writer.
comments.mathematician: Sam has not yet achieved the age-related standard in mathematics and is having additional support and provision to develop his skills. He is making progress with the key areas of number and calculation and is developing his mathematical confidence. The focus for Sam in Year 5 will be to continue building his understanding of number and place value alongside developing greater fluency with the four operations. Building fluency with multiplication tables and developing greater confidence with number and place value will be essential to supporting his progress in Year 5.
comments.learner_21c: Sam, the progress you have made as a 21st Century Learner throughout Year 4 is clear to everyone and really pleasing to see. Your friendly and open manner makes you someone who others genuinely enjoy learning with. We are looking forward to seeing even more of what you are capable of in Year 5, Sam.

You also collaborate effectively with others. The drive and determination you show across all of your learning is clear to everyone and is something to be very proud of. These are qualities to be proud of, Rory. Keep building on them in Year 5 and you will continue to go from strength to strength.
